content.problemStatement:
Chef has a grid of size **N * N** with its rows numbered from **1** to **N**, and the columns also numbered from **1** to **N**. The cell at the **i**th row and the **j**th column is cell **(i,j)**. From cell **(i,j)**, you can go in one of the four directions **U L D R** (corresponding to up, left, down, right) to cells **(i-1,j)**, **(i,j-1)**, **(i+1,j)**, **(i,j+1)** respectively, and of course you cannot go outside of the grid. However, with each cell, Chef is only allowed to go in some of the four directions (at least one), which is given by a string **directs[i][j]** containing only the characters **U L D R** denoting the directions Chef can go from cell **(i,j)**. 


Now, Chef wants to choose exactly one direction to go from each cell, so that from any cell in the grid, he can come back to same cell by following the chosen directions of the cells. Please help him!
content.inputFormat: The first line of input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N denoting the size of the grid. N lines follow. Each line contains N space-separated strings denoting the given directions for that cell.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing 'YES' if for each cell Chef can choose one and only one direction to go so that from every cell Chef can go back to that cell using chosen directions, 'NO' otherwise. If the answer is 'YES', then you must print the chosen directions in the next N lines. Each line will contain N strings. The jth string in the ith line must consist of a single character: the direction chosen for cell (i,j).

sampleTestCases.input:
2
2
RD D
UR UL
3
RD LR DL
RU LU LDU
U UL L
sampleTestCases.output:
YES
R D
U L
NO
sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, we can choose the directions R, D, U, L for the cells (1,1), (1,2), (2,1), (2,2) respectively. For the second test case, there is no way to choose the directions such that from any cell, we can come back to that cell using the chosen directions.

hidden.label: maximum boundary

1
150
RD LR DL
RU LU LDU
U UL L

5

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a graph-based approach. We can represent the grid as a directed graph, where each cell is a node and the directions are edges. We can then use a depth-first search (DFS) or breadth-first search (BFS) to traverse the graph and check if it is possible to choose one direction for each cell such that from any cell, we can come back to that cell using the chosen directions.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2 * 4^N) — explain: per query is O(N^2 * 4^N), total is O(T * N^2 * 4^N)
